title: Les jours, les mois et les saisons
description: Name days, months, and seasons; use frequency adverbs to describe habits — and learn to fill in a date of birth correctly, a required field on every TEF/TCF form.

cards.title: Les jours, les mois et les saisons

content.subtitle: Module 3 · Leçon 3
content.body: Days, months, dates, and how often you do things — this vocabulary shows up everywhere: forms, calendars, and casual conversation about your week.

content.explanation: French days of the week are never capitalized (unlike English). The week is considered to start on Monday.
Months are also lowercase in French. You'll need them constantly for dates of birth on official forms.
Adding "le" before a day means "every [day]" (a habit). No article means "this coming [day]" (one specific occurrence).

examples.text: Le lundi, je vais à la piscine.
examples.translation: On Mondays (every Monday), I go to the pool.

examples.text: Lundi, je vais à la piscine.
examples.translation: This Monday, I'm going to the pool.

content.explanation: Frequency adverbs normally go right after the conjugated verb — a common word-order mistake for English speakers, who put them before the verb.

examples.translation: I often go to the movies. (adverb AFTER the verb)

cards.title: La date de naissance sur un formulaire

content.body: Every TEF/TCF registration form asks for your "date de naissance" (date of birth), written day/month/year: "15/03/1995" is read aloud as "le quinze mars mille neuf cent quatre-vingt-quinze". Practice saying your own birth date this way.

content.prompt: Comment dit-on "I always eat breakfast" avec le bon ordre des mots ?

content.textWithBlanks: ___, je vais à la piscine. (Every Monday)
content.hint: Use the definite article for a habitual day

cards.title: Associez le mois à sa saison

content.prompt: Match each month to the season it belongs to (Northern Hemisphere).

content.statement: Les jours et les mois s'écrivent avec une majuscule en français, comme en anglais.

content.prompt: Arrange the words to say "I often go to the movies."

cards.title: Quels mots sont des adverbes de fréquence ?

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Quelle est votre date de naissance ?
lines.translation: What is your date of birth?

lines.speaker: Candidat
lines.text: Je suis né le quinze mars mille neuf cent quatre-vingt-quinze.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Et vous venez souvent en France ?
lines.translation: And do you come to France often?

lines.speaker: Candidat
lines.text: Non, rarement. C'est ma première visite.
lines.translation: No, rarely. This is my first visit.

content.prompt: Write out this date of birth in full French words: "15/03/1995".

acceptedAnswers: le quinze mars mille neuf cent quatre-vingt-quinze
quinze mars mille neuf cent quatre-vingt-quinze

content.intro: Two quick questions before the summary.

questions.prompt: Où se place l'adverbe de fréquence dans une phrase simple ?
"Le lundi" veut dire "every Monday", pas seulement "this Monday".

content.heading: Key takeaways
points: Days and months are lowercase in French
"Le + day" = habitual (every Monday); no article = a specific occurrence
Frequency adverbs (souvent, toujours, rarement...) go after the conjugated verb
Dates of birth are read as "le [jour] [mois] [année]" — a required TEF/TCF form field

content.title: Module 3 terminé !
content.message: Félicitations ! You've completed Le Quotidien — routines, time, and dates are now yours to command.
